registry.description: Wonder is the inner response of the person who has encountered something that exceeds their capacity to contain or explain — the experience of being undone and enlarged at the same time. The Hebrew and Greek vocabulary covers amazement, astonishment, the particular quality of being arrested and struck by what is greater than expected. Wonder in Scripture is not merely a pleasant aesthetic experience but a theological posture: it is the appropriate response to the works and ways of God, whose greatness and goodness continually exceed what could be anticipated.

terms.medium_def:
1) to be marvellous, be wonderful, be surpassing, be extraordinary, separate by distinguishing action
1a) (Niphal)
1a1) to be beyond one's power, be difficult to do
1a2) to be difficult to understand
1a3) to be wonderful, be extraordinary
1a3a) marvellous (participle)
1b) (Piel) to separate (an offering)
1c) (Hiphil)
1c1) to do extraordinary or hard or difficult thing
1c2) to make wonderful, do wondrously
1d) (Hithpael) to show oneself wonderful or marvellous
